key_insight: A learned control module can outperform static or logits-based token ordering by choosing better generation sequences for multimodal diffusion models.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical way to improve text-to-image alignment and multimodal reasoning without changing the base model architecture, by adding a trainable order-selection layer.
method: Train a control block to predict generation order instead of relying on model logits alone.
Optimize the controller with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO).
Use the learned ordering to guide masked diffusion generation for both image synthesis and multimodal understanding.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 4.08% relative improvement on GenEval for text-to-image alignment and 4.85% relative improvement on VLMEvalKit for multimodal understanding.
watch_item: The approach adds an extra learned component and reinforcement-style training, so performance may depend on the quality and stability of the controller training.
paper_title: Reinforcing the Generation Order of Multimodal Masked Diffusion Models
